gloss.en: thin string used for sewing

words.example: She used a needle and thread.

gloss.en: the entrance or doorway of a building

words.example: He stepped across the threshold.

gloss.en: very excited and pleased

words.example: I'm thrilled about the good news.

gloss.en: to grow strong, healthy or successful

words.example: New businesses thrive in this area.

gloss.en: regular rise and fall of the sea

words.example: We watched the tide come in.

gloss.en: to make something firmer or more secure

words.example: He turned the screw to tighten the lid.

gloss.en: wood used for building or making things

words.example: The house is built from timber.

gloss.en: happening at exactly the right time

words.example: The police made a timely arrival.

gloss.en: dried leaves used for cigarettes and pipes

words.example: The room was full of tobacco smoke.

gloss.en: willingness to accept different views or people

words.example: She had no tolerance for rude jokes.

gloss.en: to allow something you do not like

words.example: I will not tolerate such bad behaviour.

gloss.en: money paid to use a road or bridge

words.example: We paid a toll to cross the bridge.

gloss.en: highest in position

words.example: He lives on the top floor.

gloss.en: to cause severe pain to punish or force someone

words.example: The soldiers were ordered not to torture prisoners.

gloss.en: to throw something lightly or carelessly

words.example: I tossed the book aside and got up.

gloss.en: counting everyone or everything together

words.example: Their total cost was 18,000 dollars.

gloss.en: containing poison; poisonous

words.example: The factory released toxic chemicals.

gloss.en: a mark or sign that something existed

words.example: They found traces of an ancient city.

gloss.en: a company's protected name, symbol or design

words.example: The logo is a registered trademark.

gloss.en: a line of marks left by someone or something

words.example: The injured man left a trail of blood.

gloss.en: a wheeled container pulled by a vehicle

words.example: The car was towing a trailer with a boat.

gloss.en: an act of buying or selling business

words.example: The bank records every financial transaction.

gloss.en: a written copy of spoken words

words.example: She read the transcript of the interview.

gloss.en: a complete change in someone or something

words.example: The town has undergone a complete transformation.

gloss.en: the process of being moved between places

words.example: The goods were damaged in transit.

gloss.en: the act of passing something from one to another

words.example: Doctors hope to stop the transmission of the disease.

gloss.en: the quality of being clear and easy to understand

words.example: We need greater transparency in government.

gloss.en: allowing you to see through it

words.example: The insect's wings are almost transparent.

gloss.en: a mental condition caused by severe shock

words.example: She still suffers from childhood trauma.

gloss.en: a formal agreement between countries

words.example: The two nations signed a peace treaty.

gloss.en: very great in amount or degree

words.example: We heard a tremendous explosion.

gloss.en: connected with a tribe or tribes

words.example: The museum displays tribal art.

gloss.en: a court dealing with a particular problem

words.example: The case went before an international tribunal.

gloss.en: an act showing respect, often for the dead

words.example: Her friends paid tribute to her at the funeral.

gloss.en: the part of a gun you press to fire

words.example: He slowly pulled the trigger.

gloss.en: a group of three people or things

words.example: A trio of runners led the race.

gloss.en: a great success, achievement or victory

words.example: The discovery was a triumph of modern science.

gloss.en: a prize given for winning a competition

words.example: The team lifted the trophy after the final.

gloss.en: worried and anxious

words.example: She looked into his troubled face.

gloss.en: a person who controls money held in trust

words.example: The bank acts as trustee for the estate.

gloss.en: teaching, especially in small groups

words.example: She received private tuition in French.

gloss.en: the number of people who attend an event

words.example: This year's festival had a record turnout.

gloss.en: total goods or services a company sells

words.example: The company has an annual turnover of millions.

gloss.en: the action of turning something with your hand

words.example: She gave the lid another twist and it opened.

gloss.en: a student studying for a first degree

words.example: He is a first-year undergraduate.

gloss.en: important but not easily noticed

words.example: The underlying cause of the problem is money.

gloss.en: to gradually weaken confidence or authority

words.example: The defeats undermine the team's confidence.

gloss.en: used to emphasize something is certainly true

words.example: There is undoubtedly truth in what he says.

gloss.en: to join people or things into a single unit

words.example: The new leader hopes to unify the country.

gloss.en: never having happened or been known before

words.example: The situation is unprecedented in modern times.

gloss.en: to show something publicly for the first time

words.example: The Queen will unveil the new statue today.

gloss.en: going to happen soon

words.example: Everyone is talking about the upcoming election.

gloss.en: a more powerful version of a machine or system

words.example: The software needs an upgrade.

gloss.en: to support something right and keep it going

words.example: We have a duty to uphold the law.

gloss.en: a public service like electricity, water or gas

words.example: The cost of every utility has gone up.

gloss.en: to use something for a practical purpose

words.example: They utilize solar power for heating.

gloss.en: completely

words.example: We are utterly different from each other.

gloss.en: a space completely empty of air or gas

words.example: The pump creates a vacuum.

gloss.en: not clear in someone's mind

words.example: I have only a vague memory of that day.

gloss.en: the state of being legally or officially acceptable

words.example: The validity of the agreement has expired.

gloss.en: to disappear suddenly or mysteriously

words.example: He turned around and vanished into the house.

gloss.en: often changing; likely to change

words.example: The weather here is very variable.

gloss.en: of many different types

words.example: The students gave varied opinions.

gloss.en: a tube that carries blood toward the heart

words.example: The nurse found a vein in his arm.

gloss.en: a business project that involves taking risks

words.example: The new business venture lost a lot of money.

gloss.en: relating to or using words

words.example: The job needs good verbal skills.

gloss.en: an official judgement made in court

words.example: Has the jury reached a verdict?

gloss.en: to check that something is true or accurate

words.example: We have no way to verify his story.

gloss.en: writing arranged in lines with rhythm or rhyme

words.example: Most of the play is written in verse.

gloss.en: used to show two sides against each other

words.example: It is France versus Brazil in the final.

gloss.en: a tube carrying blood or liquid

words.example: He burst a blood vessel in his eye.

gloss.en: a person with much experience in an activity

words.example: He is a veteran of many campaigns.

gloss.en: able to be done; likely to succeed

words.example: This is the only viable option we have.

gloss.en: full of life and energy
gloss.zh: 充满活力的，生气勃勃的

words.example: New York is a vibrant city.

gloss.en: criminal activity involving sex or drugs

words.example: The police set up a vice squad.

gloss.en: violent and cruel

words.example: The dog made a vicious attack.

gloss.en: a person who lives in a village

words.example: Some villagers have lived here all their lives.

gloss.en: to break a law or agreement

words.example: Their actions violate international law.

gloss.en: the act of breaking a law or agreement

words.example: They were in open violation of the treaty.

gloss.en: behaviour showing high moral standards

words.example: He led a life of virtue.

gloss.en: connected with the voice

words.example: She studied vocal music for years.

gloss.en: to make a serious, formal promise

words.example: She vowed never to speak to him again.

gloss.en: the state of being weak and easily hurt

words.example: We must protect the vulnerability of newborn babies.

gloss.en: weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally

words.example: These offices are highly vulnerable to attack.

gloss.en: a hospital area for patients of one type

words.example: She works on the children's ward.

gloss.en: a building where goods are stored

words.example: There was a fire at the furniture warehouse.

gloss.en: the activity of fighting a war

words.example: They are trained in guerrilla warfare.

gloss.en: a legal document giving police authority

words.example: The police had an arrest warrant.

gloss.en: a person who fights in a battle or war

words.example: He came from a proud warrior nation.

gloss.en: to make or become less strong or powerful

words.example: The team was weakened by injury.

gloss.en: to make cloth by crossing threads together

words.example: They weave baskets from strips of willow.

gloss.en: a wild plant growing where it is not wanted

words.example: The yard was overgrown with weeds.

gloss.en: in good health

words.example: I don't feel very well today.

gloss.en: general health and happiness

words.example: Exercise improves your physical well-being.

gloss.en: not at all; not of any kind

words.example: They received no help whatever.

gloss.en: not at all; not of any kind

words.example: They received no help whatsoever.

gloss.en: by which; because of which

words.example: They use a system whereby all staff are trained.

gloss.en: while; at the same time as

words.example: It is illegal to drive whilst using a phone.

gloss.en: to hit hard with a whip

words.example: The rider began to whip the horse.

gloss.en: completely

words.example: His behaviour was wholly inappropriate.

gloss.en: to make or become wider

words.example: Her eyes widened in surprise.

gloss.en: a woman whose husband has died

words.example: The widow receives a small pension.

gloss.en: the measurement from one side to the other

words.example: The terrace runs the full width of the house.

gloss.en: being happy and ready to do something

words.example: Success depends on a willingness to learn.

gloss.en: to rub a surface to clean or dry it

words.example: Please wipe your feet on the mat.

gloss.en: the ability to say clever, funny things

words.example: She has a quick and sharp wit.

gloss.en: the act of taking money from a bank account

words.example: You can make a withdrawal of up to 250 dollars.

gloss.en: a period of physical exercise to keep fit

words.example: She does a 20-minute workout every morning.

gloss.en: the practice of showing respect for a god

words.example: The church is a place of worship.

gloss.en: worth the time, money or effort spent

words.example: It was a worthwhile cause to support.

gloss.en: deserving respect, attention or support

words.example: Few of his ideas are worthy of attention.

gloss.en: to shout loudly in anger, fear or pain

words.example: He began to yell at the other driver.

gloss.en: to produce or provide crops, profits, results

words.example: The fields yield a large crop each year.

gloss.en: a young person or a child

words.example: The camp is for youngsters aged 8 to 14.
